# `liber:display@1`

Generated reference for the `liber:display` package (`display.lsidl`). See [the LSIDL language](../../../LSIDL.md).

LiberSystem display package - capability-scoped application surfaces.

## Imports

- `error` from [`liber:base@1`](/docs/gen/liber/base/v1.md)
- `task` from [`liber:process@1`](/docs/gen/liber/process/v1.md)

## Types

### enum `pixel-format`

Pixel layouts accepted by DisplayService. The first format matches the existing virtio-gpu scanout and ConsoleService representation: in memory B, G, R, unused; as a little-endian u32, 0xXXRRGGBB.

| case | ordinal | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `b8g8r8x8` | 0 |  |

### record `surface-info`

A writable application surface. `pixels` is a shared MemoryObject: DisplayService retains a read+map duplicate and transfers a write+map handle to the client. Its wire length is exactly `pitch * height`; `pitch` is at least `width * 4` and all arithmetic is checked before allocation. The client owns the pixels, never the physical scanout.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `pixels` | `buffer` |  |
| `width` | `u32` |  |
| `height` | `u32` |  |
| `pitch` | `u32` |  |
| `format` | `pixel-format` |  |

### record `display-event`

A new preferred logical size for a native-sized surface. Fixed-size clients may ignore it and remain scaled/centered; a client that acquired `(0, 0)` releases and reacquires at the new size. Events are coalesced, so only the newest size matters.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `width` | `u32` |  |
| `height` | `u32` |  |

### record `presentation-stats`

Cumulative DisplayService presentation work. `source-pixels` is the client damage area accepted; `output-pixels` is the number of scanout pixels written by the CPU, including a first-frame clear. Times use the monotonic nanosecond clock and split CPU blit/scale work from the synchronous virtio-gpu transfer+flush acknowledgement.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `presents` | `u64` |  |
| `direct-presents` | `u64` |  |
| `scaled-presents` | `u64` |  |
| `source-pixels` | `u64` |  |
| `output-pixels` | `u64` |  |
| `blit-ns` | `u64` |  |
| `flush-ns` | `u64` |  |
| `max-present-ns` | `u64` |  |

## Interfaces

### interface `display`

One display connection owns at most one surface. `present` is synchronous: the service validates the damage rectangle, copies/scales that rectangle to its scanout and completes the device flush before replying; the client must not modify pixels inside the rectangle until the call returns. `release`, channel peer-close, or process death drops the surface and restores the console with a full repaint. The client is not told whether its surface is fullscreen; a compositor can later implement this same contract without an API break. `(0, 0)` requests the server's preferred logical size; a zero on only one axis is invalid. `events` is a live, bounded-channel stream of preferred-size changes.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `acquire` | `acquire(width: u32, height: u32) -> result<surface-info, error>` |  |
| 2 | `present` | `present(x: u32, y: u32, width: u32, height: u32) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 3 | `release` | `release() -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 4 | `events` | `events() -> stream<display-event>` |  |
| 5 | `input-focus` | `input-focus() -> result<handle<channel>, error>` | Return the one-shot proof channel for this connection's active surface. The client transfers it to `input.subscribe-keys`; a background or released surface has no proof, and changing foreground revokes the previous peer. |

### interface `display-admin`

Privileged launcher boundary. PermissionManager transfers the Process capability returned by ProcessService and receives a fresh display connection bound to that exact process. Ordinary display clients never receive this interface and cannot bind themselves or another task; emergency revocation signals the bound task.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `bind` | `bind(task: handle<task>) -> result<handle<channel>, error>` |  |
| 2 | `stats` | `stats() -> presentation-stats` |  |
